events.content:
Purpose: PTY output streaming improvements for GitHub issue #390.
STATUS: The core feature is ALREADY IMPLEMENTED. The full pipeline exists:
  - Rust: src/pty_worker.rs emits `worker_stream` frames (lines 441-444, 836-839)
  - Protocol: packages/sdk/src/protocol.ts defines `worker_stream` BrokerEvent
    and `WorkerToBroker` types
  - SDK: packages/sdk/src/relay.ts handles `worker_stream` events, provides
    agent.onOutput() callback with per-agent filtering and unsubscribe
  - Tests: packages/sdk/src/__tests__/orchestration-upgrades.test.ts covers
    onOutput, per-agent filtering, unsubscribe, and idle-resume on output

The existing push model works end-to-end:
  Rust pty_worker → send_frame("worker_stream") → Broker → SDK event handler
  → onWorkerOutput global hook + dispatchOutput() → agent.onOutput(callback)

REMAINING GAP: The Rust side emits worker_stream on EVERY PTY read chunk with no rate-limiting or buffering. High-frequency output (e.g. streaming compilation logs) floods the broker with small frames. Adding 100ms batched buffering in src/pty_worker.rs would reduce frame rate to ~10/sec max while preserving real-time feel. SDK-level output filtering by stream type (stdout vs stderr) could also be added as a convenience.
Approach: 8-step dag workflow — Parsed 8 steps, 2 parallel tracks, 6 dependent steps, DAG validated, no cycles
Workflow abandoned: Preflight check failed: Preflight check failed (exit 1)
